description:
This small Western Hardwood Sulfur Shelf mushroom is growing out of the trunk of a Carob Tree, about 10 feet up. There was a more complete mushroom further down the trunk, which I observed separately here: https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/192295499. It was being eaten by Yellow Cellar Slugs.

Host plant: https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/201517099

I've previously observed 2 of these mushrooms here in the past, so perhaps this is the same colony: https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?nelat=33.794708185211&nelng=-117.74926735770939&place_id=any&subview=map&swlat=33.79456329931911&swlng=-117.74943633687732&taxon_id=47170&user_id=kyle_eaton_photography&verifiable=any

These photos were edited to have a Custom White Balance (Photoshop's preset).

description: Rock Tree-nettle
Gymnosporia
Solanum

I usually don't take many photos of dead things that I find, hoping to find live ones eventually, but I came across 4 or 5 of these earlier on the hike and was beginning to wonder why.
Most were sitting across rocks, all posed as if they had just died on the spot while walking along.

Apparently a fungus that emerges between the segments of a millipede usually 12 to 24 hours after rain.

Observation duplicated for the millipede here: https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/256022698
